definition: existing in large quantities; plentiful
examples: The region has abundant natural resources.
Fish are abundant in this lake during spring.

definition: to gradually collect or gather over time
examples: Dust tends to accumulate on unused shelves.
She accumulated years of experience in research.

definition: extremely dry with little rainfall
examples: The arid desert receives almost no rain.
Farmers struggle to grow crops in arid regions.

definition: the layer of gases surrounding the Earth
examples: The atmosphere protects us from harmful radiation.
Pollution is damaging the upper atmosphere.

definition: something that blocks movement or progress
examples: A coral reef acts as a natural barrier against waves.
Language can be a barrier to communication.

definition: a severe snowstorm with strong winds
examples: A blizzard closed all the mountain roads.
Travelers were stranded during the blizzard.

definition: a light gentle wind
examples: A cool breeze blew in from the ocean.
We enjoyed a pleasant breeze on the terrace.

definition: a sudden disaster causing great damage
examples: The earthquake was a catastrophe for the region.
Failure to act could lead to environmental catastrophe.

definition: movement in a circular path; distribution
examples: Blood circulation keeps the body healthy.
The circulation of ocean currents affects climate.

definition: the typical weather conditions of a region
examples: The climate in this area is mild and wet.
Climate change is altering global weather patterns.

definition: the land along the edge of the sea
examples: Tourists flock to the coast in summer.
Many cities were built along the coast.

definition: to protect from harm or waste
examples: We must conserve water during the drought.
The park helps conserve endangered species.

definition: to make impure by adding harmful substances
examples: Industrial waste can contaminate rivers.
The spill contaminated the drinking water supply.

definition: hard skeletons of tiny sea animals forming reefs
examples: Coral reefs support diverse marine life.
Rising temperatures are killing coral worldwide.

definition: a large bowl-shaped hole in the ground
examples: The volcano left a massive crater.
Meteor impacts can create enormous craters.

definition: to prepare land for growing crops
examples: Farmers cultivate rice in flooded fields.
She cultivated a garden of native plants.

definition: a series of events that repeat; to go through repeatedly
examples: The water cycle moves moisture around the globe.
Nutrients cycle through the ecosystem constantly.

definition: to break down into simpler parts
examples: Leaves decompose and enrich the soil.
Organic waste decomposes faster in warm weather.

definition: the clearing of forests on a large scale
examples: Deforestation threatens biodiversity in the tropics.
Deforestation contributes to climate change.

definition: a flat area where a river splits before entering the sea
examples: Rich soil in the delta supports agriculture.
The Nile delta is one of the world's largest.

definition: closely packed together; thick
examples: The forest was too dense to walk through.
Dense fog reduced visibility to zero.

definition: a dry barren area with little vegetation
examples: Camels are well adapted to desert life.
The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert.

definition: to become progressively worse
examples: Air quality continues to deteriorate in cities.
The building's condition has deteriorated over time.

definition: a sudden event causing great damage
examples: The flood was the worst disaster in decades.
Emergency teams responded quickly to the disaster.

definition: a prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall
examples: The drought destroyed crops across the region.
Water restrictions were imposed during the drought.

definition: the study of organisms and their environment
examples: Ecology helps us understand ecosystem balance.
She earned a degree in ecology and conservation.

definition: a community of living things and their environment
examples: Wetlands are fragile ecosystems.
Pollution disrupts the entire ecosystem.

definition: the natural world or surroundings
examples: We must protect the environment for future generations.
The work environment affects employee productivity.

definition: an imaginary line dividing Earth into northern and southern halves
examples: Countries near the equator have tropical climates.
The equator receives the most direct sunlight.

definition: the gradual wearing away of soil or rock
examples: Coastal erosion threatens seaside villages.
Wind and water cause erosion over time.

definition: to change from liquid into vapour
examples: Water evaporates quickly in the desert heat.
The puddle evaporated within hours.

definition: a wide continuous area
examples: The expanse of prairie stretched to the horizon.
An expanse of ice covered the Arctic Ocean.

definition: no longer existing as a living species
examples: Dinosaurs have been extinct for millions of years.
Several species are at risk of becoming extinct.

definition: the animals of a particular region
examples: The island's fauna includes rare birds.
Researchers studied the local fauna and flora.

definition: capable of producing abundant crops
examples: The fertile valley produces excellent wine.
Rich soil makes this land highly fertile.

definition: the plants of a particular region
examples: The flora of the rainforest is incredibly diverse.
Botanists catalogued the mountain flora.

definition: a prediction of future weather
examples: The weather forecast predicts rain tomorrow.
Meteorologists forecast a cold front this week.

definition: remains of ancient plants or animals in rock
examples: Fossils reveal how life evolved on Earth.

definition: to turn into ice due to cold
examples: Lakes freeze over during harsh winters.
Water pipes can freeze and burst.

definition: the science of Earth's physical structure
examples: Geology explains how mountains were formed.
She specializes in geology and mineral resources.

definition: a slowly moving mass of ice
examples: Glaciers are melting due to global warming.

definition: the natural home of an animal or plant
examples: Wetlands provide habitat for migratory birds.
Urban sprawl destroys wildlife habitat.

definition: the gathering of crops; to gather crops
examples: The harvest was the best in ten years.
Farmers harvest wheat in late summer.

definition: having a high amount of moisture in the air
examples: The climate is hot and humid in summer.

definition: a violent tropical storm with strong winds
examples: A hurricane devastated the coastal towns.
Residents evacuated before the hurricane arrived.

definition: a large floating mass of ice
examples: Only a small part of an iceberg is visible above water.

definition: to supply water to land for crops
examples: Farmers irrigate fields using canal systems.
Desert farms rely on irrigated water.

definition: all the visible features of an area of land
examples: The mountain landscape took our breath away.

definition: distance north or south of the equator
examples: The city lies at a latitude of 35 degrees north.

definition: a flash of bright light during a thunderstorm
examples: Lightning struck the tree during the storm.
Stay indoors during lightning storms.

definition: great size or importance
examples: The earthquake reached a magnitude of 7.2.
We did not grasp the magnitude of the problem.

definition: soft wet land covered with grass
examples: Birds nest in the coastal marsh.
The marsh filters pollutants from the water.

definition: the science of weather and atmosphere
examples: Meteorology uses satellites to track storms.
Advances in meteorology improve storm warnings.

definition: to move from one region to another seasonally
examples: Birds migrate south for the winter.
Whales migrate thousands of miles each year.

definition: a naturally occurring solid substance
examples: The rock contains valuable minerals.
The body needs minerals like iron and calcium.

definition: small amounts of water present in the air or ground
examples: Plants absorb moisture from the soil.
High moisture levels promote mould growth.

definition: a seasonal wind bringing heavy rain
examples: The monsoon brings relief after the dry season.
Flooding is common during the monsoon season.

definition: a fertile spot in a desert with water
examples: Travelers rested at an oasis in the desert.
The park was an oasis of green in the city.

definition: a very large body of salt water
examples: The Pacific is the largest ocean on Earth.
Ocean currents regulate global temperatures.

definition: the curved path of a celestial body; to move in a path
examples: The moon's orbit around Earth takes 27 days.
Satellites orbit the planet at high speed.

definition: a gas essential for breathing
examples: Plants produce oxygen through photosynthesis.
Divers carry tanks of compressed oxygen.

definition: a form of oxygen that protects Earth from UV rays
examples: The ozone layer shields us from harmful radiation.
Chemicals have damaged the ozone layer.

definition: a piece of land almost surrounded by water
examples: Korea is located on a peninsula.
The peninsula juts out into the Mediterranean.

definition: the process by which plants make food using sunlight
examples: Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide into oxygen.
Without photosynthesis, life on Earth would not exist.

definition: a flat elevated area of land
examples: The Tibetan Plateau is called the roof of the world.
The city sits on a high plateau.

definition: to make air, water, or land dirty and harmful
examples: Factories pollute rivers with chemical waste.
Car exhaust pollutes the air in cities.

definition: rain, snow, or other water falling from the sky
examples: Annual precipitation in this region exceeds 100 cm.

definition: to keep something in its original state
examples: National parks preserve wilderness areas.
We must preserve biodiversity for future generations.

definition: a dense forest with heavy rainfall
examples: The Amazon rainforest produces much of the world's oxygen.

definition: able to be replaced naturally and not depleted
examples: Solar and wind are renewable energy sources.

definition: a large natural or artificial lake for storing water
examples: A reservoir supplies water to the entire city.
The reservoir was built to prevent flooding.

definition: a supply of something useful
examples: Water is our most precious natural resource.
The country exports mineral resources abroad.

definition: relating to the countryside rather than the city
examples: Rural communities depend on agriculture.
Many young people leave rural areas for cities.

definition: a grassy plain in tropical regions
examples: Lions hunt on the African savanna.
The savanna has a wet and dry season.

definition: one of the four divisions of the year
examples: Spring is my favourite season.
Each season brings different weather patterns.

definition: matter that settles at the bottom of liquid
examples: Rivers carry sediment to the ocean.
Geologists study sediment to learn about past climates.

definition: relating to earthquakes or earth vibrations
examples: Seismic activity increased before the eruption.

definition: relating to the sun
examples: Solar panels convert sunlight into electricity.
Solar energy is clean and abundant.

definition: a group of similar organisms that can reproduce
examples: Over a thousand species live in this forest.
Many species face extinction due to habitat loss.

definition: violent weather with strong wind and rain
examples: A powerful storm knocked down trees.
The storm caused widespread power outages.

definition: a particular type of material with uniform properties
examples: Water is the most common substance on Earth.
The chemist identified an unknown substance.

definition: to continue to live despite difficult conditions
examples: Only the hardiest plants survive in the desert.
Few businesses survived the economic crisis.

definition: to keep something going over time
examples: We need policies that sustain economic growth.
Forests sustain countless species of wildlife.

definition: wetland permanently saturated with water
examples: Alligators live in the Florida swamp.
The swamp teems with insects and birds.

definition: the degree of heat or cold
examples: Temperatures drop sharply at night.
Global temperatures have risen over the past century.

definition: a stretch of land with particular physical features
examples: The terrain was too rugged for vehicles.
Soldiers trained on mountainous terrain.

definition: to melt ice or frozen food; to become less cold
examples: The sun thawed the frozen ground.

definition: the regular rise and fall of sea level
examples: The tide comes in twice a day.

definition: a violently rotating column of air
examples: A tornado destroyed several homes.
Tornado warnings were issued across the state.

definition: poisonous or harmful to living things
examples: Toxic chemicals leaked from the factory.
Some mushrooms are toxic if eaten.

definition: relating to the hot humid regions near the equator
examples: Tropical rainforests are rich in biodiversity.

definition: an enormous ocean wave caused by an earthquake
examples: A tsunami struck the coast after the earthquake.

definition: a vast treeless Arctic region
examples: Reindeer graze on the Arctic tundra.

definition: a tropical cyclone in the northwest Pacific
examples: A typhoon battered the Philippines.
Residents boarded up windows before the typhoon.

definition: a low area between hills or mountains
examples: The valley was filled with wildflowers.
A river runs through the fertile valley.

definition: plants in a particular area
examples: Dense vegetation covers the hillside.
Fire destroyed miles of vegetation.

definition: a mountain that erupts lava and ash
examples: The volcano erupted after decades of dormancy.
Ancient civilizations built cities near volcanoes.

definition: a cascade of water falling from a height
examples: We hiked to a spectacular waterfall.
The waterfall plunges over 100 metres.

definition: an area of land that drains into a river
examples: The entire watershed was protected as a reserve.

definition: an uncultivated uninhabited region
examples: They camped deep in the wilderness.

definition: wild animals and plants
examples: Wildlife populations have declined sharply.

definition: moving air, especially outdoors
examples: Strong wind knocked over the fence.

definition: existing in thought rather than physical form
examples: Justice is an abstract concept.
The painting features abstract shapes and colours.

definition: detailed examination of elements or structure
examples: Statistical analysis revealed a clear trend.
The report includes a thorough analysis of the data.

definition: something that deviates from what is standard
examples: The temperature reading was an anomaly.

definition: equipment designed for a particular purpose
examples: The laboratory has modern scientific apparatus.
Firefighters use breathing apparatus in smoke.

definition: something accepted as true without proof
examples: His argument is based on a false assumption.

definition: the smallest unit of a chemical element
examples: Everything is made up of atoms.
Scientists split the atom in the twentieth century.

definition: a quality or characteristic; to regard as caused by
examples: Curiosity is an important attribute in research.
She attributed the error to faulty equipment.

definition: an unfair preference for or against something
examples: Confirmation bias affects scientific judgement.

definition: a mathematical determination of an amount
examples: A simple calculation shows the answer is wrong.
Complex calculations require powerful computers.

definition: a chemical element found in all living things

Diamonds are made of pure carbon.

definition: a substance that speeds up a chemical reaction
examples: The enzyme acts as a catalyst in digestion.

definition: the smallest structural unit of a living organism
examples: Every cell contains genetic material.

definition: an enclosed space or room
examples: The combustion chamber ignites the fuel.
The heart has four chambers.

definition: relating to chemistry; a substance produced by a chemical process
examples: Chemical reactions release energy.
Household chemicals should be stored safely.

definition: a substance formed from two or more elements
examples: Water is a compound of hydrogen and oxygen.

definition: an abstract idea or general notion
examples: The concept of gravity was revolutionary.
She explained the concept clearly to students.

definition: a judgement reached after consideration
examples: The study's conclusion supports our hypothesis.
We drew the conclusion that the method works.

definition: to carry out; to allow heat or electricity to pass through
examples: Scientists conduct experiments to test theories.
Metals conduct electricity efficiently.

definition: based on what is generally accepted
examples: Conventional medicine treats symptoms with drugs.

definition: standards used to judge or decide something
examples: The criteria for admission include test scores.
Candidates must meet strict selection criteria.

definition: extremely important or essential
examples: Timing is crucial to the success of the experiment.
Good communication is crucial in teamwork.

definition: facts and statistics collected for analysis
examples: Researchers analysed the data carefully.
The data supports our original hypothesis.

definition: to reach a conclusion by reasoning
examples: From the evidence, we can deduce the cause.

definition: to show clearly; to prove by evidence
examples: The experiment demonstrates how heat transfers.

definition: to obtain from a source; to come from
examples: The word derives from Latin.
Many medicines derive from plants.

definition: to discover or notice something
examples: Sensors detect movement in the room.
The test can detect the virus early.

definition: to plan or invent by careful thought
examples: Engineers devised a new filtration system.

definition: a measurable extent such as length or width
examples: The problem has a social as well as economic dimension.

definition: to recognize a difference; to treat unfairly

definition: to move from the usual place; to replace

definition: clearly different or separate
examples: The two species are genetically distinct.

definition: to pull or twist out of shape; to misrepresent

definition: the way something is spread over an area

definition: showing a great deal of variety
examples: The city has a diverse population.

definition: an area of knowledge or activity

definition: characterized by constant change and activity

definition: to completely remove or get rid of

definition: based on observation or experiment rather than theory

definition: power derived from physical or chemical resources

definition: to improve the quality or value of something

definition: something that exists as a single unit

definition: equal in value or function

definition: to set up on a permanent basis; to prove

definition: to roughly calculate; an approximate calculation

definition: to develop gradually over time

definition: to be greater than a particular amount

definition: to deliberately leave out

definition: stated clearly and in detail

definition: to use for benefit; a bold act

definition: to remove or take out; a short passage

definition: a circumstance that contributes to a result
examples: Age is an important factor in recovery.

definition: having limits or bounds

definition: a mathematical rule or method
examples: The formula calculates compound interest.

definition: the basis on which something is built

definition: a basic structure underlying a system

definition: the purpose of something; to work properly

definition: forming a necessary base or core
examples: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right.

definition: to produce or create something
examples: The turbine generates electricity.

definition: a proposed explanation to be tested
examples: The hypothesis was confirmed by the experiment.

definition: to recognize or establish what something is
examples: Researchers identified the gene responsible.

definition: extremely large or great
examples: The project required immense effort.

definition: a strong effect; to have a strong effect on

definition: implied though not directly expressed
examples: There was an implicit agreement between them.

definition: to suggest something without stating it directly
examples: His tone implied that he was unhappy.

definition: to point out or show
examples: Symptoms indicate a possible infection.

definition: a single person; separate and distinct

definition: to bring about or give rise to

definition: to deduce from evidence and reasoning

definition: to hinder or restrain an action or process

definition: existing at the beginning; the first letter

definition: a new method, idea, or product

definition: an accurate deep understanding

definition: to combine parts into a whole

definition: to act in a way that affects another

definition: coming between two stages or levels

definition: to explain the meaning of something

definition: the basic physical systems of a country or organization

definition: to carry out research or inquiry

definition: to cite or appeal to as authority

definition: an individual article or unit

definition: a paid position of regular employment

definition: a daily record; a scholarly publication
examples: She keeps a journal of her travels.

definition: to show to be right or reasonable

definition: a sheet or thickness of material
examples: Earth's atmosphere has several layers.

definition: an educational talk; to give such a talk
examples: The professor gave a lecture on genetics.

definition: permitted by law

definition: to make or enact laws

definition: open to new ideas; generous

definition: reasoning conducted according to strict principles

definition: to keep in good condition; to assert

definition: important or serious; a main subject

definition: the edge or border; a small difference

definition: fully developed; to reach full development

definition: the greatest possible amount

definition: a system of parts working together

definition: means of mass communication

definition: to intervene between disputing parties

definition: relating to medicine or treatment of disease

definition: to leave one's own country to settle permanently in another

definition: to take part in an activity
examples: Students participate in community service.

definition: strong and barely controllable emotion

definition: a person of the same age or status; to look closely

definition: relating to the immediate surroundings of something
examples: Ambient music played softly in the café.
Ambient temperature affects device performance.

definition: to fasten or join
examples: Attach the file to your email.
Strong bonds attach families together.

definition: an aggressive action; to act aggressively
examples: The attack caused widespread panic.
Critics attacked the policy fiercely.

definition: to succeed in achieving
examples: She attained her career goals.
The team attained record sales.

definition: an effort to achieve; to try
examples: His attempt failed.
She attempted to climb the peak.

definition: to be present at; to pay attention
examples: Over 200 people attended the lecture.
Please attend to the instructions.

definition: a settled way of thinking or feeling
examples: A positive attitude helps success.
Public attitude toward smoking has changed.

definition: to draw interest or attention
examples: The museum attracts tourists.
Bright colours attract insects.

definition: the action of regarding something as being caused by a person or thing
examples: Proper attribution is required in academic writing.
The attribution of the quote was incorrect.

definition: a public sale to the highest bidder
examples: The painting sold at auction.
They auctioned the estate.

definition: the assembled spectators or listeners
examples: The audience applauded loudly.
The show draws a young audience.

definition: a writer of a book or article
examples: The author signed copies of her novel.
He is the author of ten books.

definition: the power to give orders; an expert
examples: Local authority approved the plans.
She is an authority on linguistics.

definition: working by itself with minimal human control
examples: The door has an automatic opener.
Breathing is automatic.

definition: able to be used or obtained
examples: Tickets are available online.
The doctor is available tomorrow.

definition: typical; the result of adding quantities and dividing
examples: The average age is thirty-five.
Temperatures average 20°C in July.

definition: to keep away from
examples: Avoid fatty foods for better health.
She avoided answering the question.

definition: a prize; to give a prize
examples: She won an award for excellence.
The jury awarded damages to the plaintiff.

definition: having knowledge or perception
examples: Be aware of the risks.
I was not aware of the change.

definition: in the interest of a person or group
examples: She spoke on behalf of the team.
I accept the award on his behalf.

definition: the way a person acts
examples: Good behaviour is expected.
Animal behaviour fascinates biologists.

definition: an acceptance that something is true
examples: Religious belief varies worldwide.
It is my belief that education transforms lives.

definition: an advantage; to be helpful to
examples: Exercise has many health benefits.
The policy benefits small businesses.

definition: preconceived opinion not based on reason
examples: We must fight racial prejudice.
Prejudice against immigrants persists.

definition: a connection; a financial certificate
examples: Shared goals create strong bonds.
Government bonds are low-risk investments.

definition: short in duration; a summary
examples: The meeting was brief.
The lawyer submitted a legal brief.

definition: to grant a title or benefit; to discuss
examples: The university conferred an honorary degree.
Experts conferred on the best approach.

definition: a serious disagreement; to clash
examples: The conflict lasted three years.
Their schedules conflict on Tuesdays.

definition: to make unable to think clearly
examples: Similar names confuse customers.
Do not confuse correlation with causation.

definition: a formal meeting; a national legislature
examples: The congress meets annually.
Congress passed the new law.

definition: a word connecting clauses; the action of joining
examples: Use a conjunction to link sentences.
The event occurred in conjunction with the festival.

definition: an estimate of income and expenditure
examples: The government announced the budget.
We budget carefully each month.

definition: the mass or main part of something
examples: Buy rice in bulk to save money.
The bulk of the work is done.

definition: having the ability to do something
examples: She is capable of leading the team.
The software is capable of complex analysis.

definition: the maximum amount something can contain
examples: The stadium capacity is 40,000.
She works at full capacity.

definition: wealth; a city serving as seat of government
examples: Startups need venture capital.
London is the capital of England.

definition: to take by force; the act of capturing
examples: The photo captures the moment perfectly.
Police captured the suspect.

definition: a class or division of things
examples: Books are sorted by category.
Which category does this fall into?

definition: to come to an end
examples: Hostilities ceased at midnight.
The factory ceased production.

definition: a difficult task; to question
examples: The role presents a real challenge.
She challenged the decision in court.

definition: a means of communication or passage
examples: Use the proper channel for complaints.
The channel connects two lakes.

definition: the mental qualities of a person; a symbol
examples: Honesty is part of his character.
Chinese characters are complex.

definition: a price; to ask payment; to accuse
examples: There is no charge for delivery.
Police charged him with fraud.

definition: a graphical representation; to map
examples: The chart shows sales trends.
We charted a course to the island.

definition: a person trained in chemistry; a pharmacist
examples: The chemist analysed the sample.
Buy aspirin at the chemist.

definition: a fact or condition connected with an event
examples: Under the circumstances, we cancelled.
Financial circumstances forced a sale.

definition: to quote as evidence
examples: She cited three studies in her paper.
The judge cited previous rulings.

definition: relating to ordinary citizens; polite
examples: Civil rights must be protected.
They maintained a civil discussion.

definition: to state as a fact; an assertion
examples: He claims innocence.
Insurance claims rose this year.

definition: to make clear or understandable
examples: Could you clarify your position?
The update clarified the rules.

definition: judged over time to be of highest quality
examples: It is a classic example of irony.
Shakespeare wrote many classics.

definition: a distinct section of a legal document
examples: Read every clause in the contract.
A relative clause adds information.

definition: a person using professional services
examples: The lawyer met with her client.
The firm has many corporate clients.

definition: relating to climate
examples: Climatic conditions vary by latitude.
Climatic change affects crop yields.

definition: a place giving medical treatment
examples: She visited a dental clinic.
The clinic offers free vaccinations.

definition: a system of rules or symbols
examples: Follow the dress code.
Programmers write computer code.

definition: logical and consistent
examples: She gave a coherent explanation.
The essay lacks a coherent structure.

definition: to occur at the same time
examples: The events coincided exactly.
Our views coincide on this issue.

definition: to fall down suddenly; a sudden failure
examples: The bridge collapsed in the storm.
The company faced financial collapse.

definition: a person with whom one works
examples: My colleague helped with the report.
She discussed it with senior colleagues.

definition: to gather or accumulate
examples: Collect data from all sources.
She collects rare stamps.

definition: a country under foreign control; a group of settlers
examples: India was once a British colony.
Ant colonies have complex structures.

definition: to join or merge
examples: Combine the ingredients in a bowl.
The firms combined to form one company.

definition: a state of physical ease; to soothe
examples: Live in comfort and safety.
She comforted the crying child.

definition: an order; to give orders
examples: He gave a clear command.
She commands a unit of soldiers.

definition: a remark; to express an opinion
examples: Comments are welcome.
Critics commented favourably on the film.

definition: the activity of buying and selling
examples: E-commerce transformed retail.
International commerce drives growth.

definition: a group appointed to investigate; to order work
examples: A commission was set up to review policy.
An artist was commissioned for the mural.

definition: to carry out or pledge to do
examples: Nations committed to reduce emissions.
He committed a serious error.

definition: a group appointed for a specific function
examples: The committee meets every month.
She chairs the finance committee.

definition: a raw material or primary product
examples: Oil is a valuable commodity.
Commodity prices fluctuate daily.

definition: to share or exchange information
examples: We communicate via email.
Dolphins communicate through sounds.

definition: a group living in one place
examples: The community rallied after the flood.
Online communities connect people globally.

definition: to estimate similarities and differences
examples: Compare prices before buying.
The study compares two methods.

definition: to make up for loss or suffering
examples: Insurance will compensate victims.
Nothing can compensate for the loss.

definition: to strive against others
examples: Firms compete for market share.
Athletes compete in the Olympics.

definition: to collect information into a list or book
examples: She compiled a list of references.
The data was compiled from surveys.

definition: something that completes; to enhance
examples: The wine complements the meal.
Skills complement each other well.

definition: consisting of many parts; a group of buildings
examples: The issue is highly complex.
They live in a housing complex.

definition: a part of a larger whole
examples: Each component was tested separately.
Trust is a key component of teamwork.

definition: to write or create; to constitute
examples: Beethoven composed nine symphonies.
Water is composed of hydrogen and oxygen.

definition: to flatten by pressure; to reduce in size
examples: Compress the file before sending.
The machine compresses waste into blocks.

definition: complete and including everything
examples: The report provides comprehensive coverage.
She has comprehensive insurance.

definition: to consist of; to make up
examples: The course comprises ten modules.
Women comprise half the workforce.

definition: to calculate using a machine
examples: Computers compute vast amounts of data.
We computed the total cost.

definition: to form an idea; to become pregnant
examples: It is hard to conceive of life without internet.
She conceived her first child at thirty.

definition: to focus attention; to gather in one place
examples: Concentrate on the main points.
Industry concentrates in urban areas.

definition: the action of forming an idea; the start of pregnancy
examples: The conception of the plan took months.
From conception to birth takes nine months.

definition: worry; a matter of interest; to worry
examples: Safety is our main concern.
The issue concerns all citizens.

definition: to bring to an end; to reach a decision
examples: The study concluded that exercise helps.
We conclude the meeting at five.

definition: solid and real; building material
examples: Give concrete examples in your essay.
The building is made of reinforced concrete.
